paper_title: Archaeological and Environmental Analysis at El Arenal de la Virgen

methods.description: A comprehensive fieldwork method for expanding excavation areas and recording archaeological findings using advanced technology.

steps.description: A new phase of fieldwork was developed in 2017, following an open-area strategy which considerably expanded the excavation area from 6 to 84 m².
steps.searchable_summary: expand excavation area from 6 to 84 m²

steps.description: The excavation followed the 2007 fieldwork grid system and datum (497.77 m a.s.l.), based on 1-m² excavation squares.
The location of each finding was recorded three-dimensionally using the ARCH·e System Fieldwork, a wireless data recording network.
The sediment was dry sieved through a 5-and 2-mm mesh on site.
steps.searchable_summary: dry sieve sediment through 5 and 2 mm mesh

steps.description: A selection of sediment samples together with the fill of occupation features were wet sieved and floated for recovery of macro-botanical remains.
steps.searchable_summary: wet sieve and float sediment samples

steps.description: During the excavation, the sedimentary units and archaeological features were independently recorded as numbered Strata and Stratigraphic Units (SUs) using the Harris system.
steps.searchable_summary: record sedimentary units using Harris system

steps.description: For each SU, the excavation process was documented by digital photography and the morphometric dimensions recorded by photogrammetry.
steps.searchable_summary: document excavation with photography and photogrammetry

methods.description: A method for processing archaeological sediments to recover and analyze macro-botanical remains.

steps.description: About 2025 litres of archaeological sediments were processed with a flotation machine to recover macro-botanical remains.
After drying, samples were additionally sieved through a column of 4-, 2-, 1-, 0.5-and 0.25-mm meshes.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve dried samples through mesh column

steps.description: The obtained fractions of each flotation sample were subsequently sorted using a binocular microscope.
Charcoal analysis is based on the botanical identification of carbonized wood.
The frequency of the taxa identified was measured as a percentage of fragment counts.
steps.searchable_summary: measure taxa frequency by fragment count

steps.description: The seeds were identified according to metric and morphological criteria.
steps.searchable_summary: identify seeds by metric and morphology

methods.description: Analysis of the spatial distribution and morpho-technical characteristics of lithic assemblages.

steps.description: The spatial distribution showed an uneven scattering pattern across the excavation area.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze spatial distribution of lithic remains

steps.description: Performed a Clark-Evans aggregation test to test the unevenness of the lithic assemblage's spatial distribution.
Morpho-technical analysis of the lithic assemblage showed a predominance of micro-debris.
Detailed techno-typological analysis revealed differences between Phase 1 and Phase 2.
